# Contributing

Thanks for your interest in contributing to the App Store Connect Terraform provider. Issues and pull requests are welcome.

## Reporting issues

- Search [existing issues](https://github.com/elevenode/terraform-provider-appstore/issues) first.
- Open a bug report or feature request using the provided templates.
- For security vulnerabilities, do **not** open a public issue — see [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md).

## Development

Requires Go (see `go.mod` for the version) and Terraform.

```bash
go build ./...   # build
go vet ./...     # vet
gofmt -l .       # check formatting (should print nothing)
go install .     # install the provider locally for manual testing
```

To try the provider locally, add a `dev_overrides` block to `~/.terraformrc`:

```hcl
provider_installation {
  dev_overrides {
    "elevenode/appstore" = "<your GOBIN, e.g. /Users/you/go/bin>"
  }
  direct {}
}
```

### Tests

```bash
go test $(go list ./... | grep -v '/internal/appstore/test') -count=1   # unit tests (offline)
```

Integration tests under `internal/appstore/test/` hit the live App Store Connect API and require credentials (`APPSTORE_KEY`, `APPSTORE_KEY_ID`, `APPSTORE_KEY_ISSUER_ID`). They run in CI on non-Dependabot PRs and on the nightly schedule; you generally don't need them for a local change.

### Documentation

Files under `docs/` are generated by [`tfplugindocs`](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-docs) from `examples/` and the schema descriptions — do **not** edit them by hand. If your change touches `schema.go`, `examples/`, or a resource/data-source description, regenerate and commit the result:

```bash
tfplugindocs generate
```

## Pull requests

- Branch from `main` using a `feature/`, `fix/`, or `chore/` prefix.
- Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) for commit messages (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `build(deps):`, …).
- Keep the PR focused and make sure `go build`, `go vet`, `gofmt`, and unit tests pass.
- Regenerate docs if applicable.

By contributing, you agree that your contributions are licensed under the project's [Apache 2.0 license](../LICENSE).
